name: Chocolate Peanut Butter Cheesecake (2)
recipeInstructions:
1. PREHEAT OVEN TO 350°F.
2. Grease and flour an 8-inch by 4-inch loaf pan or any 6-cup loaf or fluted tube pan.
3. In a medium mixing bowl, whisk together the cocoa and water until smooth. Allow to cool to room temperature and lightly whisk in the vanilla extract and eggs.
4. In a large mixing bowl, combine the remaining dry ingredients (flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt) and mix on low speed for 30 seconds to blend. Add half of the chocolate mixture and the butter. Mix on low speed until the dry ingredients are moistened.
5. Increase speed to medium (high if using a hand mixer) and beat for 1 minute to aerate and develop the cake's structure.
6. Scrape down the sides, then gradually add the remaining chocolate mixture in two batches, beating 20 seconds after each addition to incorporate the ingredients and strengthen the structure.
7. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the surface with a spatula. The batter will be almost 1/2 inch from the top of a 4-cup pan. If your pan is slightly smaller, use any excess batter for cupcakes.
8. Bake for 50 to 60 minutes (40 to 50 minutes in a fluted tube pan) or until a wooden toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Tent loosely with buttered foil after 20 minutes to prevent overbrowning.
9. Let the cake cool in the pan on a rack for 10 minutes. Loosen the sides with a metal spatula and invert onto a greased wire rack. If baked in a loaf pan, reinvert so that the top is up and cool completely before wrapping airtight.
There is a coffee syrup recipe that goes with this for extra moistness and a subtle coffee accent:
1. In a small pan, stir together 1/4 cup WATER and 2 tablespoons SUGAR. Bring to a full rolling boil.
2. Cover and remove from heat. When cool, add 1 tablespoon KAHLA (coffee liqueur).
3. Brush half the syrup onto the top of the cooled cake. Let it sit for 10 minutes before inverting the cake onto a lightly greased rack.
4. Brush the bottom and sides with remaining syrup. Reinvert onto the rack, top side up, to finish cooling. The coffee flavor stays in the background, accentuating the chocolate.
